detailed_description.heading: Durable cable for internet connections
detailed_description.text: The UniFi Ethernet Patch Cables, offered by Ubiquiti, is a high-performance networking cable designed to provide reliable and high-speed connectivity for a wide range of Ubiquiti’s UniFi devices and other network equipment. Those cables are specifically crafted to meet the rigorous demands of both commercial and residential network environments.

detailed_description.heading: Durable cable for internet connections
detailed_description.text: Constructed from high-quality materials, cables features Category 6 (Cat6) specifications, which support data transfer speeds of up to 1 Gbps (Gigabits per second) and a bandwidth of up to 250 MHz. This makes it suitable for applications such as high-speed data transfer, streaming, and online gaming where high bandwidth and lower latency are crucial.

detailed_description.heading: Durable cable for internet connections
detailed_description.text: UniFi Ethernet Patch cables are available in lengths of 0.1, 0.3, 1, 2, 3, 5 or 8 meters and comes in a sleek black, blue and white color variations. The connectors on each end are RJ45 with an ultra-thin outer diameter of 3 mm, universally compatible with most Ethernet ports found on network devices. Additionally, the UniFi Ethernet Patch Cables are designed with a durable PVC (Polyvinyl Chloride) jacket that enhances the cable's longevity and resistance to various environmental factors like temperature variations and humidity. The cable is also flexible, facilitating easy installation and routing through tight spaces without risk of damaging the cable.

detailed_description.heading: Durable cable for internet connections
detailed_description.text: Overall, the UniFi Ethernet Patch Cable is an ideal solution for networking needs within the Ubiquiti ecosystem and beyond, providing both durability and high performance to maintain stable and efficient network connections. The patch cables meet the technical standard for commercial building cabling for telecommunications products and services TIA-568 and passed the TIA-568-B.2 Cat6 channel test by Fluke Networks.
